Off Calendar Oct-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NON-PARTY WITNESS AMPCO SYSTEM PARKING, A SUBSIDIARY OF ABM INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOITON TO QUASH NON-PARTY SUBPOENA DUCES TECUM.

APPEARANCES: Sarah A. Hodgson, Esq. of Metzger Law Group 562/437-4499 for pltfs & opposing parties; Victor Russame, Esq. of Watten, Discoe, Bassett & McMains 714/542-5400 for moving party; Patrick Foley, Esq. of Steptoe & Johnson 213/439-9400 for deft Chevron USA; Ian P. Culver, Esq. of Dongell Lawrence Finney LLP 213/943-6100 for deft Bank of America; Maureen A. Rodgers, Esq. of Glynn & Finley, LLP 925/210-2800 for deft ConocoPhillips; (all parties appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED IN PART. AMPCO MUST APPEAR AT THE DEPOSITION AND PRODUCE RECORDS IN ITS POSSESSION, IF ANY, FOR THE TIME PERIOD 1995-1998 WHEN DECEDENT WORKED FOR THE COMPANY. AMPCO MUST RESPOND TO QUESTIONS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. In addition, the Court limits the deposition to two hours on the record and that it occur at Ampco's corporate headquarters. Moving party to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

Off Calendar Sep-30-2010 Continued To Oct-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
NON-PARTY WITNESS, AMPCO SYSTEM PARKING, A SUBSIDIARY OF ABM INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH NON-PARTY SUBPOENA DUCES TECUM.

APPEARANCES: Victor Russame, Esq. of Watten, Discoe, Bassett & McMains 714/542-5400 for moving party (appearing via CourtCall); Jennifer Bonneville, Esq. of Steptoe & Johnson 213/439-9400 for deft Chevron USA (appearing via CourtCall).
